messages.author: swaits
messages.text: Hey all. Looking for someone in your area interested in experimenting with some stuff I’ve been developing. It starts with this: https://github.com/swaits/donglora And I’ve built a multiplexer and pretty nice p2p bridge on top of that. Basically you flash and run the firmware. Connect to USB, optionally run the mux if you want to connect more than one thing to your donglora. Run bots, analytics, bridges, whatever. Anyway donglora basically gives you really simple, raw access to the radio. The bridge I put together uses Iroh for p2p. So two people wanting to bridge just need to exchange a few public keys. Iroh does the rest. Any packets received on one side are sent to the peers who can transmit them on their side. OBTW I’m in Northern Utah, which is why I am seeking anyone interested in experimenting with this with in Nevada. Comfort in Linux needed, familiarity with Rust is helpful but not essential. LMK!!!
